mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities through lower discount rates and wider risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is moving down rather than flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is declining month-over-month, or if the sign of the effect flips for more than one 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month pace, real rates are pressured upward and equity valuation multiples compress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and raises recession odds, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in a late-cycle expansion for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS reflects resilient income formation and supports consumption, so sustained monthly job gains should lift equities over the following quarter through stronger revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive payroll-growth periods do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than negative payroll-growth periods, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad domestic demand and housing-related activity, which should improve revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-high demand signal does not outperform either single-series proxy alone, or if positive 20-day returns fail to dominate when both series are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in INDPRO growth with steady or rising CPIAUCSL implies stagflation pressure from constrained supply, which should depress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns negative while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if stagflation-triggered windows do not show lower-than-baseline SPX returns, or if the signal is positive in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth relative to prior quarters indicates accelerating aggregate demand and better earnings expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth rises above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-growth GDPC1 quarters are not followed by above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the market direction in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL should hurt equities through both discount-rate pressure and inflation uncertainty, making forward S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation is rising
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, rising-inflation episodes fail to underperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution, or if the signal turns positive in a tightening cycle.
